zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor de Scales於1315年出生在英國諾福爾可米德萊頓時，她的父親Robert de Scales 2nd Baron Scales36歲，母親Egeline Courtenay36歲。她和John de Sudeley 2nd Baron Sudeley至少有3個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1361年6月6日在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾素德勒享年46歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eleanor de Scales于1315年出生在英国諾福爾可米德萊頓时，她的父亲Robert de Scales 2nd Baron Scales36岁，母亲Egeline Courtenay36岁。她和John de Sudeley 2nd Baron Sudeley至少有3个儿子和3个女儿。她去世于1361年6月6日在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾素德勒享年46岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Scales
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: plural form of   Scale  , naming a herdsman living and working on a summer pasture, or a habitational name for someone from a place called Scales, mostly minor places in Lancashire and Yorkshire.   Scholes   is a dialect variant of the same name.

(of Norman origin): habitational name either from Écalles-Alix (Seine-Maritime), Écalles (in Estouteville-Écalles, Seine-Maritime), or from Escalles (Pas-de-Calais). The earliest spellings of these names suggest a derivation from Latin scala, scalae (plural) ‘ladder, staircase, stile’, perhaps applied to a steep slope or path, although Old Norse skáli (‘herdsman's hut, shieling’, see Scale ) has also been suggested for Écalles. Challis may be a variant of the same name. The surname has also been established in southern Ireland (Limerick) since the 14th century.

variants.name: Scholes
meanings: English: habitational name from one or other of the places called Scholes in Lancashire and Yorkshire, or from Scole in Norfolk, all named with Middle English scole or scoles ‘herdsman's hut(s)’ (Old Norse skáli). In northern England the name was sometimes interchangeable with Scales , a dialect variant of the same word.

variants.name: Scale
meanings: English: from northern Middle English scale ‘herdsman's hut, shed, shieling’ (Old Norse skáli). The surname may have denoted a herdsman who lived and worked on a summer pasture or someone from any of several mostly minor places so named in Lancashire and Yorkshire. The name often varies between singular and plural forms; see Scales .

variants.name: Schooley
meanings: English (Sussex): variant of Scholey, a habitational name either from Scholey's Bridge in Hemsworth (Yorkshire) or the lost Scholey in Rastrick (Yorkshire), recorded in that spelling in 1399. The placenames derive from Old Norse skáli ‘(temporary) hut, shed, shieling’ + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’. Compare Scales .

variants.name: Schoolcraft
meanings: English: variant of Scowcroft, from Middle English scale, sc(h)ole ‘hut, shed’ (Old Norse skáli) + croft ‘small enclosed field’. The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a field containing a hut, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Scowcroft in Oldham (Lancashire) and Scholecroft in Morley (Yorkshire). Compare Scales and Schofield .

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor de Scales was born in 1315, in Middleton, Norfolk, England, her father, Robert de Scales 2nd Baron Scales, was 36 and her mother, Egeline Courtenay, was 36. She had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with John de Sudeley 2nd Baron Sudeley. She died on 6 June 1361, in Sudeley, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eleanor de Scales est née en 1315, à Middleton, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Scales 2nd Baron Scales, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Egeline Courtenay, avait 36 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles avec John de Sudeley 2nd Baron Sudeley. Elle est décédée le 6 juin 1361, à Sudeley, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 46 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Eleanor de Scales 1315 in Middleton, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert de Scales 2nd Baron Scales 36 und ihre Mutter Egeline Courtenay 36. Sie hatte mit John de Sudeley 2nd Baron Sudeley mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 6. Juni 1361 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Sudeley, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor de Scales è nata il nel 1315 nel Middleton, Norfolk, England, sua padre, Robert de Scales 2nd Baron Scales, aveva 36 anni e sua madre, Egeline Courtenay, aveva 36 anni. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie con John de Sudeley 2nd Baron Sudeley. Morì il 6 giugno 1361, nel Sudeley, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 46 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Scales(이)가 1315년 영국 노르폴크 미들레턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Scales 2nd Baron Scales(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Egeline Courtenay(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 John de Sudeley 2nd Baron Sudeley(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1361년 6월 6일 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 수데리에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor de Scales nasceu em 1315, em Middleton, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Scales 2nd Baron Scales, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Egeline Courtenay, tinha 36 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas com John de Sudeley 2nd Baron Sudeley. Ela faleceu em 6 de junho de 1361, em Sudeley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 46 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eleanor de Scales родилась в 1315 г. в местности миддлетон, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert de Scales 2nd Baron Scales было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Egeline Courtenay, 36. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери от John de Sudeley 2nd Baron Sudeley. Она умерла 6 июня 1361 г. в местности судели, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 46 лет.

Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas con John de Sudeley 2nd Baron Sudeley. Murió el 6 de junio de 1361, en Sudeley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 46 años.
